title: Unused method parameters should be removed
description: A typical code smell known as unused function parameters refers to parameters declared in a function but not used anywhere within the function’s body. While this might seem harmless at first glance, it can lead to confusion and potential errors in your code. Disregarding the values passed to such parameters, the function’s behavior will be the same, but the programmer’s intention won’t be clearly expressed anymore.
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Find method parameters that are never used in the method body

conditions: Parameter is never referenced in method body

logic.reasoning: Unused parameters make code confusing and indicate possible bugs.

should_trigger: private void DoSomething(int a, int b) { Console.WriteLine(a); }
